enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully. Use the word in brackets to form a new word that fits logically and grammatically in the blank. Pay attention to prefixes (un-, in-, im-, dis-) and suffixes (-tion, -sion, -ment, -ness, -ful, -less, -ive, -ous).

questions.texte: The government's ______ (corrupt) is a major obstacle to development in many countries.
It is ______ (respect) to interrupt someone while they are speaking.
After hours under the hot sun, the farmer felt ______ (weak) and had to rest.
Vaccines are a very ______ (effect) way to prevent many infectious diseases.
The information on that website is completely ______ (accurate); you cannot trust it.
Her ______ (happy) was evident when she received the scholarship for her outstanding academic performance.
The students showed great ______ (responsible) by cleaning up the classroom without being asked.
The climb up Mount Cameroon is ______ (danger) if you do not have an experienced guide.
His repeated failure to submit his assignments led to his final ______ (suspend) from school.
It is ______ (possible) for one person to solve all the community's problems alone.

enonce.texte: Read the paragraph about a young Cameroonian entrepreneur below. Complete the text by transforming the words in brackets to create a coherent and meaningful story. Then, answer the questions based on the completed text.

questions.texte: Complete the text with the correct form of the words in brackets.

sousQuestions.texte: A lack of ______ (employ) opportunities often leads to ______ (frustrate) among young people in urban areas.
However, one young man from Douala showed remarkable ______ (create) by starting an online business that recycles plastic waste into ______ (use) building materials.
His ______ (innovate) solution has had a significant ______ (improve) on local pollution levels.
Many people were initially ______ (sure) about his project, but its success proved them wrong.

questions.texte: Based on the completed text, explain in one sentence how the young man's action helped the environment.
questions.reponse: His action helped the environment by recycling plastic waste into useful building materials, which reduced local pollution.

questions.texte: Identify the negative prefix used in the completed text and name the base word.
questions.reponse: The negative prefix is 'un-' in the word 'unsure'. The base word is 'sure'.

intitule: Analyzing Word Formation for Civic Engagement

enonce.texte: Read the short speech about 'Good Governance' given by a student council president. Then, analyze the word formation choices and answer the critical thinking questions.

questions.texte: Speech excerpt: "The re-election of a leader should not be automatic. We must show our disagreement with any form of mismanagement or injustice. A responsible citizen is never indifferent to the suffering of others. Our collective happiness depends on the transparency of our actions."

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two words in the speech that use a prefix meaning 'not' or 'opposite of'. What is the base word for each?
Find a word in the speech that uses a suffix to create an abstract noun (an idea or quality). What is the base word and the suffix?
sousQuestions.reponse: Possible answers: 'mismanagement' (base: manage, suffix: -ment), 'injustice' (base: just, suffix: -ice), 'happiness' (base: happy, suffix: -ness), 'transparency' (base: transparent, suffix: -cy).

questions.texte: Imagine you are a citizen. Using the word 'responsible,' add a prefix to create its antonym. Then, use both the original and the new word in two separate sentences to explain their importance in a democracy.
questions.reponse: Antonym: irresponsible. Sentence 1 (responsible): A responsible citizen always votes in elections. Sentence 2 (irresponsible): It is irresponsible to spread false information about a candidate.
